mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly compress equity discount rates and lower corporate borrowing costs, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) or Rank IC falls below 0.02 during rate decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 4.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, causing investors to sell equities and produce negative forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances when unemployment exceeds the 4.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining retail sales and stagnant housing starts indicates fragile consumer demand and weak residential investment, driving negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term between retail sales and housing starts yields a Rank IC above 0.04, or forward returns remain positive during divergence episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Deceleration in industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects tightening capacity constraints and weakening manufacturing demand, leading to negative medium-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Industrial production deceleration fails to predict negative 60-day returns, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels exceeding 285.0 trigger expectations of aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or flat when CPI exceeds 285.0, and the signal produces a negative information coefficient over any 24-month period.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls supports household income and corporate revenue projections, creating a persistent upward bias in S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows zero predictive power for 60-day returns, with a t-statistic below 1.5 in out-of-sample regressions across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in real GDP and slowing payroll growth confirms a broad-based demand shock, forcing equity repricing and generating negative short-term returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns, with accuracy below 45 percent during confirmed demand contraction periods.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production rebounding above 104.0 following a sharp decline signals restored manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are negative or statistically insignificant when industrial production crosses the 104.0 threshold during recovery phases.
